repository.name: abbrs
repository.description: Fast and safe abbreviation expansion for zsh

commit.message:
refactor: remove suffix match conflict detection

Suffix match (e.g. "g" matching "ffmpeg") provided no practical value
since kort only expands at command position — typing "ffmpeg" never
triggers the "g" abbreviation. This removes SuffixPathMatch, the
--strict CLI flag, and the warnings field from ConflictReport. The
settings.strict config field is kept for backward compatibility but
is now ignored.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add `kort init zsh` subcommand for shell integration

Replace unit `Init` command with subcommand-based `Init { Zsh, Config }`.
`kort init zsh` outputs the zsh integration script to stdout via
`include_str!`, enabling `eval "$(kort init zsh)"` in .zshrc without
manual file placement. `kort init config` preserves the previous
config template generation behavior.

Also fixes:
- Error messages referencing old `kort init` updated to `kort init config`
- Deduplicate config existence checks via `require_config()`
- UTF-8 safe truncation in `cmd_list` (prevents panic on multibyte chars)
- Migrate tests from deprecated `Command::cargo_bin()` to `cargo_bin_cmd!()`

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix session cookie behind reverse proxy (trust proxy)

Fly.io terminates TLS at its proxy. Without trust proxy, Express
doesn't see requests as HTTPS, so secure cookies are never set/sent.

feat: v1.2 — Deeper Analysis (reviews, monorepo, semantic diff, spec diff) (#6)

* feat: add PR review parsing and monorepo detection

Review parsing:
- Fetches review comments and reviews from GitHub API
- Computes approval status (approved/changes_requested/pending/mixed)
- Extracts key concerns from reviewer comments
- Groups inline comments by file with discussion topics
- 7 tests covering all review states and comment grouping

Monorepo detection:
- Detects workspace directories (packages/, apps/, libs/, etc.)
- Recognizes workspace config files (pnpm-workspace.yaml, turbo.json, etc.)
- Identifies affected packages from changed file paths
- Supports npm, pnpm, Turborepo, Lerna, Nx, Cargo workspaces
- 8 tests covering detection patterns and deduplication

Both features render in Markdown summary and PR comment outputs.
71 tests total across 7 test suites.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add semantic diff analysis for intent extraction

Extracts structured meaning from patch data:
- Detects function, class, import, export, and type changes
- Multi-language support: TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust
- Classifies changes as added/removed/modified
- Deduplicates identical semantic entries
- Renders in Markdown output with +/- indicators
- 10 tests covering all languages and edge cases (81 total)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add spec versioning and diff comparison

Compare two prompt specs to track how a PR evolves:
- Detects changes in title, change type, files, stats, risks
- Tracks risk flag additions and resolutions
- Monitors review approval status transitions
- Structured SectionDiff output for programmatic consumption
- 9 tests covering all diff scenarios (90 total)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(llm-router): add asyncio request queue for concurrent agent traffic

When all local providers are at capacity, incoming requests now park in
an in-process queue instead of immediately returning 503. Requests wait
for a slot-freed signal (asyncio.Event pulse from track_request) with
a configurable timeout and queue depth cap.

- acquire_provider_slot() wraps select_provider_and_model with queue logic
- Slot event pulsed (set→clear) on every track_request exit
- Client disconnect detection via request.is_disconnected() each poll cycle
- Queue depth exposed in /health (queue_depth, queue_max_depth)
- get_queue_status() for monitoring
- Tunable: QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH (default 20), QUEUE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS (default 120)
- providers.yaml: add qwen3.5-35b-a3b model on gaming-pc-3090

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: tighten risk classifier to eliminate false positives (#3)

E2E testing against real-world PRs (Next.js, Deno, Flask, GitHub Docs)
revealed false-positive risk flags on docs, configs, and schema files.

Changes:
- Add isDocOrTestPath() guard to auth, permissions, payment, security rules
- Require sensitive context (secret, credential, db) for config → secrets
- Require DB ORM context (prisma, drizzle, etc.) for schema → database
- Narrow permissions to rbac/acl/access-control/policy patterns
- Require SQL patterns for destructive-operations detection
- Fix scope path formatting: only glob 3+ segment paths
- Tighten constraint heuristic for schema-related DB migrations
- Add 7 regression tests covering false-positive scenarios (49 total)

[LINTEL] Exclude lock files from diff and increase truncation limit

Lock files (bun.lock, uv.lock, etc.) were consuming the entire 10K diff
budget, hiding actual code changes. Now excluded via pathspec. Truncation
limit increased to 50K for both implement and review stages.

commit.message:
feat: ground placement hints in real page content via HTML fetching

Before generating placement hints and copy snippets, all unique source
page URLs are now fetched in parallel (up to 10 concurrent requests).
BeautifulSoup strips nav/header/footer/scripts and extracts up to
1500 chars of body paragraph text per page.

When content is available the AI receives the actual page text and:
- placement_hint quotes the verbatim opening of the target paragraph
  (Ctrl+F ready for the editor to locate it instantly)
- copy_snippet rewrites that exact paragraph with [anchor text]()
  as a drop-in replacement

Pages that fail to fetch (404, JS-rendered, timeout) fall back
gracefully to URL/topic-based inference — no disruption to the run.

Adds beautifulsoup4 dependency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Config: markdownlint MD060 style=consistent

- .markdownlint.json: MD060 style=consistent elimina tutti i 1512 errori

commit.message:
Security hardening and add Instagram social link

Security fixes:
- Block javascript: URIs in about_content (XSS)
- Escape icon fields in public and admin HTML rendering (XSS)
- Restrict upload MIME types to explicit allowlist (no SVG)
- Add secure flag to session cookie in production
- Validate gallery URLs start with http(s)://
- Path traversal protection on gallery delete
- Email format validation on contact form
- Rate limit password change endpoint
- Input size limits on JSON/urlencoded bodies
- Icon field length validation
- Force password change on first login with default credentials
- Pin GitHub Action to version tag

Features:
- Add Instagram link (clemmons_nc_troop731_girls) to Social Media section
- Rename "Private Facebook Groups" to "Social Media"

auto-populate property fields without manual re-entry.

- lib/divar.ts: extractDivarToken, fetchDivarListing (8s timeout), mapDivarToFileData (fully defensive, never throws), parsePersianInt
- lib/validations/divar.ts: Zod schema enforcing divar.ir URLs
- POST /api/import/divar: auth-gated, 400 on bad URL, 502 on Divar error, always returns partial DivarImportResult
- DivarImporter component: collapsible strip with URL input, spinner, error display, CDN photo thumbnails (amber "may expire" label)
- FileForm: renders DivarImporter in create mode, merges imported fields via form.reset(), green summary banner, yellow ring-2 highlight on contacts section and address input for fields needing manual completion
- types/index.ts: DivarImportResult interface
- 26 unit tests (extractDivarToken, parsePersianInt, mapDivarToFileData)

feat: pre-deployment security hardening

- Add security headers to next.config.ts: CSP (enforced prod / report-only dev), HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy
- Add HTTP→HTTPS redirect via x-forwarded-proto (prod only)
- Add isActive DB check in middleware for immediate session invalidation on deactivate/force-logout
- Add in-memory rate limiter (lib/rate-limit.ts): login 10/15min/IP, SMS 10/min/office
- Add localhost-only guard on cron route (x-forwarded-for check)
- Document security controls in CLAUDE.md section 23

commit.message:
Amélioration triangulation : contour dense de référence

Le worker retourne un contour dense (~500 pts) fidèle au vrai bord
du dessin (CHAIN_APPROX_NONE + resample arc-length). Le resample
du contour se fait toujours depuis cette référence dense, garantissant
que les points restent sur le vrai bord quelle que soit la densité.

commit.message:
feat: three stories deployed — Cartographer's Slab, Three Readers, Six Tri-Junctions

Stories #14–16, all from the substrate diversity experiment (WP-0030).
The Cartographer's Slab introduces Mehra and the slab above the Tirthan
gorge. The Three Readers translates three AI lineages into three
draughtsmen's studies of the same valley. The Six Tri-Junctions carries
the Thread Walker from the Parvati headwall to the Karakoram — six
places where three valleys converge, six ways of saying the same thing.

20 illustrations (matplotlib chalk-on-slate and parchment), story
workspaces with notes, keys, and generate_images.py scripts.

Deployed to mayalucia.dev.

commit.message:
Reduce token costs, loosen risk params, fix dashboard fees and parser errors

- Cut API costs ~60-70%: cycle interval 5→15min, sentiment cache 5→15min,
  drop 1h candles, trim context (trades 10→5, skip correlation <2 positions),
  compress sentiment prompt 3×, cap max_tokens at 1500
- Loosen risk params: position 25%, exposure 85%, daily loss 10%, weekly 20%,
  drawdown kill switch 35%, max 50 trades/day
- Add Fee column to dashboard Recent Trades table
- Fix close path fee calculation (was hardcoded to 0.0)
- Fix decision parser: normalize null/"ALL" asset on no_trade/hold decisions
- Update all test fixtures and assertions for new risk parameters
